files.runtime_impact: File read/write for run events (filesystem only, no DB)
files.note: No session_factory dependency; suitable for memory backend

methods.name: create
methods.args: run_id, thread_id, rating, user_id=AUTO, message_id=None, comment=None
methods.isolation: async with self._sf() as session: session.add + commit + refresh

methods.name: get
methods.isolation: async with self._sf() as session: session.get + optional user_id check

methods.name: upsert
methods.isolation: SELECT then INSERT or UPDATE + commit

methods.name: aggregate_by_run
methods.user_id_resolution: None (aggregate, no user filter on count)
methods.note: Uses SQL func.count + func.sum with case expressions

cand010_feedback_repository_contract.rating_validation: rating must be +1 or -1; raises ValueError otherwise

memory_backend_handling.session_factory_none_impact: FeedbackRepository.__init__ stores None; all methods call self._sf() which raises AttributeError
memory_backend_handling.required_fallback: deps.py must provide MemoryFeedbackStore or None when session_factory is None
memory_backend_handling.note: FeedbackRepository has no MemoryRunStore equivalent in upstream; deps.py must decide fallback behavior

user_id_three_state.AUTO: resolve_user_id(user_id, method_name) → reads from contextvar or raises RuntimeError

methods.name: put
methods.args: run_id, thread_id, assistant_id, user_id=AUTO, status, multitask_strategy, metadata, kwargs, error, created_at, follow_up_to_run_id

methods.name: update_run_completion
methods.args: run_id, status, total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens, total_tokens, llm_call_count, lead_agent_tokens, subagent_tokens, middleware_tokens, message_count, last_ai_message, first_human_message, error
methods.note: Called by background worker on run completion; no user_id check

methods.name: aggregate_tokens_by_thread
methods.returns: dict with total_tokens, total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens, total_runs, by_model, by_caller
methods.note: GROUP BY model_name; uses func.coalesce for null-safe aggregation

_safe_json_contract.purpose: Ensure obj is JSON-serializable before storing in JSON columns
_safe_json_contract.fallback_chain: None → None, primitives → verbatim, dict/list → recursive, has model_dump → try model_dump(), has dict → try dict(), else → str()

_row_to_dict_contract.purpose: Convert RunRow to dict matching RunStore interface
_row_to_dict_contract.transforms: metadata_json → metadata, kwargs_json → kwargs, datetime → ISO string

memory_backend_handling.deps_py_pattern: if sf is None: app.state.run_store = MemoryRunStore()
memory_backend_handling.note: MemoryRunStore already implements RunStore interface — drop-in replacement

cand012_db_run_event_store_contract.init_signature: def __init__(self, session_factory: async_sessionmaker[AsyncSession], *, max_trace_content: int = 10240) -> None

seq_assignment_contract.mechanism: SELECT MAX(seq) + 1 WHERE thread_id with FOR UPDATE lock
seq_assignment_contract.put_single: async with session.begin(): get max_seq with_for_update(), insert with seq
seq_assignment_contract.put_batch: async with session.begin(): get max_seq once, increment for each event
seq_assignment_contract.sqlite_note: with_for_update() is no-op on SQLite; UNIQUE(thread_id, seq) constraint catches races
seq_assignment_contract.postgres_note: FOR UPDATE lock serializes concurrent seq assignments within same thread

truncate_trace_contract.action: Truncate bytes at max_trace_content, decode with errors='ignore'

_user_id_from_context_contract.purpose: Soft read of user_id from contextvar for write paths
_user_id_from_context_contract.returns: str(user.id) if user is not None else None
_user_id_from_context_contract.uuid_coercion: User.id is UUID → str required because run_events.user_id is VARCHAR and aiosqlite cannot bind UUID to VARCHAR
_user_id_from_context_contract.background_worker_behavior: Returns None (contextvar unset) → no user_id stamp on events from background workers
_user_id_from_context_contract.http_request_behavior: Returns str(user.id) set by auth middleware

content_serialization_contract.dict_content: JSON-serialized with json.dumps, metadata stamped content_is_dict=True
content_serialization_contract.deserialization: On read, if content_is_dict flag set, json.loads() to restore dict

memory_backend_handling.note: DbRunEventStore requires session_factory; no built-in memory fallback
memory_backend_handling.config_pattern: if backend='memory': event_store = JsonlRunEventStore() else: event_store = DbRunEventStore(session_factory)

cand013_jsonl_run_event_store_contract.init_signature: def __init__(self, base_dir: str | Path | None = None) -> None

seq_management.load_behavior: _ensure_seq_loaded() scans all *.jsonl files in thread_dir on first access
seq_management.increment: _next_seq() increments counter and returns new seq
seq_management.no_persistence: Counters are in-memory only; lost on process restart
seq_management.no_cross_process_isolation: Multiple processes writing to same thread will have independent counters (potential seq collision)

write_contract.record_keys: thread_id, run_id, event_type, category, content, metadata, seq, created_at

read_contract._read_thread_events: Reads ALL run files for thread, sorts by seq ascending
read_contract._read_run_events: Reads single run file, sorts by seq ascending
read_contract.malformed_lines: Logged at debug level, skipped (no exception)

list_messages_tradeoff.slow_path: list_messages(thread_id) — scans ALL run files for thread (seq ordering across runs)
list_messages_tradeoff.reason: Messages from multiple runs need unified seq ordering for display

methods.name: list_messages
methods.note: Must scan all run files for thread

session_factory_dependency_contract.description: CAND-010, CAND-011, CAND-012 all take async_sessionmaker in __init__

cand010_feedback.none_handling: No None check; would raise AttributeError if sf is None
cand010_feedback.fallback_needed: deps.py must provide alternative when sf is None

feedback_repo.required_decision: deps.py must provide fallback (None, or in-memory implementation)
feedback_repo.note: FeedbackRepository has no memory equivalent in upstream; implementation decision needed

thread_store.when_none: Unknown — ThreadMetaStore memory implementation may not exist
thread_store.note: CAND-013 thread_meta persistence not in scope for R241-22D

jsonl_retention_contract.no_rotation: No log rotation, no size limits, no automatic cleanup
deletion_triggers: delete_by_thread(thread_id) — deletes all run files for thread
delete_by_run(thread_id, run_id) — deletes single run file

retention_recommendation.note: Not specified in upstream; production deployment should consider:
options: Periodic cleanup of old thread directories (TTL-based)
Archive to cold storage before deletion

disk_space_risk.mitigation: Monitor disk usage; implement cleanup policy
disk_space_risk.trace_truncation: DbRunEventStore truncates trace content at 10KB; JsonlRunEventStore does not

put_single_transaction.scope: Single event insert with seq assignment
put_single_transaction.lock: FOR UPDATE on SELECT MAX(seq) WHERE thread_id
put_single_transaction.sqlite_behavior: FOR UPDATE is no-op; UNIQUE(thread_id, seq) constraint catches duplicate seq
put_single_transaction.atomicity: Full transaction — rollback on any failure

put_batch_transaction.scope: All events in batch, same thread assumed
put_batch_transaction.lock: FOR UPDATE on SELECT MAX(seq) once for entire batch
put_batch_transaction.optimization: One lock acquisition for N events vs N lock acquisitions

read_transactions.pattern: async with self._sf() as session: result = await session.execute(stmt)
read_transactions.autocommit: Each read opens and closes a session
read_transactions.consistency: Session-local reads; eventual consistency with concurrent writes

write_paths_without_user_id: put() — uses contextvar (None for background workers)
put_batch() — uses contextvar (None for background workers)

concurrent_write_safety.db_store: FOR UPDATE lock + UNIQUE constraint on (thread_id, seq)
concurrent_write_safety.jsonl_store: No locking — in-process _seq_counters only; multi-process unsafe

orphan_event_prevention.db_store: user_id NULL for pre-auth events; contextvar stamps new events
orphan_event_prevention.jsonl_store: No user_id concept in JSONL records

data_corruption_prevention.db_store: SQLAlchemy session.commit() atomic; FK constraints via foreign_keys=ON
data_corruption_prevention.jsonl_store: Append-only JSONL; corruption only if write is interrupted mid-line (partial line)

rollback_on_failure.db_store: Transaction rollback on exception; session.__aexit__ handles
rollback_on_failure.jsonl_store: Append mode — partial writes possible if process crashes mid-write

cases: DbRunEventStore and JsonlRunEventStore both implement RunEventStore
memory backend: JsonlRunEventStore vs DbRunEventStore selection

implementation_order_after_cand009.description: After CAND-009 (engine.py) is implemented, repositories follow this order

sequence.reason: FeedbackRepository — depends on FeedbackRow model (already in models/__init__.py)
RunRepository — depends on RunRow model; MemoryRunStore fallback available
DbRunEventStore — depends on RunEventRow model + session_factory
JsonlRunEventStore — no DB dependency; DB-independent alternative for memory backend

implementation_order_after_cand009.parallelization: Step 1 and Step 2 can run in parallel (FeedbackRepository and RunRepository are independent)
implementation_order_after_cand009.critical_path: Step 3 (DbRunEventStore) and Step 4 (JsonlRunEventStore) are independent of each other but both need ORM models

stop_conditions.action: Do not implement repositories — all depend on CAND-009 session_factory

stop_conditions.condition: Authorization scope does not expand to allow persistence/ or runtime/events/store directories

stop_conditions.action: No MemoryFeedbackStore in upstream; deps.py decision needed before implementation

rollback_cleanup_requirements.description: If partial implementation occurs, cleanup requirements
rollback_cleanup_requirements.if_feedback_repo_created: Remove feedback table operations; no persistent state to clean
rollback_cleanup_requirements.if_run_repo_created: Remove runs table operations; MemoryRunStore has no disk state
rollback_cleanup_requirements.if_db_event_store_created: Remove run_events table operations; no cleanup needed
rollback_cleanup_requirements.if_jsonl_store_created: Delete .deer-flow/threads/ directory if event files were written
rollback_cleanup_requirements.if_session_factory_used: close_engine() in deps.py shutdown hook releases all connections
rollback_cleanup_requirements.no_schema_migration_needed: create_all is idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)

feedback_memory_fallback.issue: FeedbackRepository has no upstream MemoryFeedbackStore equivalent
options: Provide None (feedback operations no-op when backend=memory)
Implement in-memory FeedbackRepository with dict storage
Delegate to a stub that raises NotImplementedError
feedback_memory_fallback.recommendation: Option 1 (None/no-op) — simplest, consistent with backend=memory philosophy
